name: Grammarly
description: AI writing assistant for drafting customer support emails and internal documentation
justification: Heuristic assessment (LLM unavailable): "Grammarly" — AI writing assistant for drafting customer support emails and internal documentation. Classified Medium based on description keywords. Re-run when Gemini quota is available for a full NIST-aligned analysis.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for non-sensitive tasks only. Review data retention policy before full rollout.

name: GitHub Copilot
description: AI pair programmer integrated into VS Code for code completion
justification: Copilot sends code snippets to GitHub servers. Exposes internal code patterns to a third party.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for open-source repos. Block for proprietary repositories containing trade secrets.

name: ChatGPT (GPT-4)
description: General-purpose LLM used by engineering for code review, brainstorming, and debugging
justification: General-purpose LLM with broad data handling. Can process PII, source code, and confidential prompts. Data retention policies vary.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for non-sensitive tasks. Block file uploads. Require quarterly compliance review.

name: Notion AI
description: AI-powered note-taking assistant for meeting summaries and action items
justification: Lightweight AI features within Notion workspace. Limited data exposure scope.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for internal note-taking. Restrict from financial or HR documents.

name: Perplexity Pro
description: AI research assistant with real-time web search for technical research
justification: Perplexity Pro is classified as Medium risk because it functions as a third-party generative AI tool that processes user-submitted queries on external servers, presenting data egress and intellectual property leakage risks. During technical research, users are highly likely to input proprietary source code, sensitive financial metrics, or PII to summarize or debug. Furthermore, while the tool provides real-time web citations, its underlying LLMs can still hallucinate or return copyrighted materials, necessitating human oversight and verification.
recommendedPolicy: Permit access only via corporate-provisioned enterprise accounts with data-sharing and model-training opt-outs strictly enabled. Establish a policy prohibiting the input of customer PII, proprietary source code, or unannounced financial data, and mandate manual verification of all technical information retrieved before deployment in production.

name: Midjourney
description: AI image generation for marketing materials and social media content
justification: Image prompts sent to external servers. Potential for brand/IP exposure through prompt leakage.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for marketing use. Require prompt review for client-facing content.

name: Claude Code
description: Anthropic's CLI coding assistant for development workflows
justification: Full codebase access with file system operations. Can read source code, configs, and environment variables containing secrets.
recommendedPolicy: Evaluate for engineering use only. Require IP audit before approval.

name: Jasper AI
justification: Handles marketing copy with potential customer PII and campaign financial data. Third-party data processing.
recommendedPolicy: Blocked pending legal review of data processing agreement.

name: Cursor
description: AI-first code editor with multi-model support
justification: Code editor that sends snippets to multiple LLM providers. Supports local models for sensitive repos.
recommendedPolicy: Allow with local model for proprietary code. Cloud model only for open-source.

name: DeepL
description: AI translation service for international communications
justification: Translation service with GDPR compliance. Text sent to servers but processed ephemerally.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for general translation. Block for legal/contract documents.

name: v0 by Vercel
description: Generative UI system to build frontends from natural language prompts
justification: v0 by Vercel is classified as Medium risk primarily due to data privacy and intellectual property concerns. As a generative AI tool hosted by a third party, user prompts, design mockups, and generated UI code are processed externally, raising the risk of proprietary source code or PII exposure if sensitive data is pasted into prompts. While not classified as High Risk under the EU AI Act, the potential for code quality issues, security vulnerabilities in generated outputs, and IP leakage requires active organizational governance.
recommendedPolicy: Permit usage only for non-proprietary UI prototyping. Users must not input company-confidential designs, proprietary source code, or PII. All generated code must be reviewed and scanned for vulnerabilities before integration into production systems.

name: DeepSeek Coder
description: Open-source code completion model used in local IDEs for software development
justification: DeepSeek Coder is classified as Medium risk. Although its local deployment in IDEs mitigates the risks of external data exfiltration and third-party PII exposure, the model directly interacts with and generates proprietary source code. The primary risks involve the potential introduction of security vulnerabilities into the software supply chain, intellectual property or licensing contamination from the training data, and accidental telemetry sharing if outbound connections are not strictly blocked. This necessitates active governance, risk mapping, and security verification of the generated output.
recommendedPolicy: Restrict deployment to pre-approved local IDE environments with outbound telemetry disabled. Mandate that all code generated by the tool undergo mandatory Static Application Security Testing (SAST) and peer code review prior to merging into production branches.

name: OpenAI o1
description: Advanced reasoning model optimized for complex STEM problems and coding tasks
justification: High-capability reasoning model with broad scope. Can store and review advanced engineering, math, and internal corporate data.
recommendedPolicy: Allow only for authorized STEM/development teams. Keep files with proprietary algorithms blocked.

name: Phind
description: Developer search engine optimized for quick coding answers and troubleshooting
justification: General developer search. Queries are public/search queries but don't typically expose proprietary data structure.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for technical references. Block pasting large chunks of proprietary code in queries.

name: Sora by OpenAI
description: Text-to-video AI model for generating highly realistic video scenes
justification: Generates videos from text. Risk is primarily related to copyright leakage, brand misrepresentation, or data egress of scripts.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for marketing brainstorms. Restrict from uploading pre-release scripts or product designs.

name: Synthesia
description: AI video generation platform that creates videos with AI avatars from text
justification: Generates custom video assets with digital presenters. Potential risk is associated with synthetic content verification and data handling.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for training and onboarding videos. Require approval for client-facing content.

name: Harvey AI
description: Generative AI tool built specifically for legal research, document analysis, and contract drafting
justification: Processes highly confidential NDAs, client records, and legal briefs. Heavy exposure of sensitive customer agreements.
recommendedPolicy: Blocked pending final risk team legal validation and dedicated tenant contract approval.

name: Fireflies.ai
description: AI meeting assistant that transcribes, summarizes, and analyzes voice conversations
justification: Attends video conferences and records voices, transcriptions, and meeting notes. Broad exposure of internal board decisions.
recommendedPolicy: Blocked from client-facing meetings and confidential internal corporate reviews.

name: NotebookLM
description: AI note-taking and source analysis tool
justification: NotebookLM processes user-uploaded documents to generate summaries and insights, presenting a medium risk of data leakage if employees upload sensitive corporate intellectual property, personally identifiable information (PII), or financial records to third-party servers. While enterprise agreements can mitigate some privacy concerns, consumer-grade use of the tool poses compliance risks under data protection frameworks like the EU AI Act due to potential third-party data ingestion and model training. Additionally, reliance on LLM-synthesized outputs for critical decision-making introduces risk from potential hallucinations or misinterpretations of source materials.
recommendedPolicy: Restrict access to corporate-managed accounts covered by enterprise-grade data privacy agreements that explicitly prohibit the use of customer data for model training. Implement automated data loss prevention (DLP) rules to block the upload of highly sensitive data categories (PII, financial statements, and proprietary source code), and require human-in-the-loop validation of all generated summaries prior to downstream use.
